title: Can't see files copied into container
body:
Hi,

It's not possible to see the files copied into `/var/www/html`. This directory specifically is a volume:

https://github.com/docker-library/wordpress/blob/master/Dockerfile-debian.template#L28

How to reproduce the issue:

```sh
$ sudo ./buildah --debug copy wordpress-working-container-2 https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/README.md /var/www/html/
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay"
DEBU[0000] overlay: override_kernelcheck=true
DEBU[0000] backingFs=extfs,  projectQuotaSupported=false
DEBU[0000] saving "https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/README.md" to "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/282ba936372f577cbee0be367ac7c14011a2cc3af07dc1e3697515ef5bdb58ff/merged/var/www/html/README.md"

$ sudo ./buildah --debug copy wordpress-working-container-2 https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/README.md /var/www/
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay"
DEBU[0000] overlay: override_kernelcheck=true
DEBU[0000] backingFs=extfs,  projectQuotaSupported=false
DEBU[0000] saving "https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/README.md" to "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/282ba936372f577cbee0be367ac7c14011a2cc3af07dc1e3697515ef5bdb58ff/merged/var/www/README.md"

$ sudo ./buildah run wordpress-working-container-2 bash
root@mrsdalloway:/var/www/html# ls
.  ..
root@mrsdalloway:/var/www/html# ls /var/www -Z
system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c485,c498 README.md  system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c485,c498 html
```

Some details:

* If I `buildah commit` the container and use `docker run` to check the images's content, I can see the files there.

* This is what I see in `journalctl -f` when I execute `buildah run wordpress-working-container-2 bash`: https://gist.github.com/bertinatto/ab182d85a5eae0edf24778d451d2774c

comments.body: We know we have an SELinux issue with buildah 1.8 which prevents writing to the volume.  But I would not expect any content written to the volume to show up in the image?

The problem seems to be the VOLUME command in the Dockerfile.  I've shrunk the Dockerfile that @bertinatto sent down a lot and can replicate the issue easily.  This also is replicating #334 easily, I don't know if they're related or not.

```
# cat Dockerfile
FROM fedora 
VOLUME /var/www/html

# buildah bud -t fed .
STEP 1: FROM fedora
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:a8ee583972c2295bb76704d4defe5116d5e4dd7ba3767aaa2cc8fcf71088ee06
 82.80 MiB / 82.80 MiB [===================================================] 12s
Copying config sha256:422dc563ca3260ad9ef5c47a1c246f5065d7f177ce51f4dd208efd82967ff182
 2.29 KiB / 2.29 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: VOLUME /var/www/html
STEP 3: COMMIT containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.override_kernel_check=true]docker.io/library/fed:latest

# ctrid=$(buildah from fed)
# buildah copy $ctrid temp.txt /var/www/html

# buildah run $ctrid bash
error resolving mountpoints for container: error populating directory "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/a760f3e10f2a7b544b66868495e5d9725a9ef5a820a2407c8aa2f4cdd2e04683/userdata/buildah-volumes/69dd2b02e1f3a65918182048ea4e29979a849d8942e8f53ed20a4bf10e529b36" for volume "/var/www/html" in container "a760f3e10f2a7b544b66868495e5d9725a9ef5a820a2407c8aa2f4cdd2e04683" using contents of "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/5cc9203b32fab9efef4d0d5f98598bff1e9847903a026c4ceebedb2ec50c0637/merged/var/www/html": Can't copy a directory

# buildah run $ctrid bash
[root@mrsdalloway /]# ls /var/www/html
[root@mrsdalloway /]# exit

# buildah copy $ctrid temp.txt /var/www
# buildah run $ctrid bash
[root@mrsdalloway /]# ls /var/www/html
[root@mrsdalloway /]# ls /var/www     
html  temp.txt
```

I tried creating a Fedora container without the Dockerfile and ran into a perm issue:

```
#buildah from fedora

# buildah run -v "/var/www/html:/var/www/html" fedora-working-container /bin/bash
[root@mrsdalloway /]# ls
bin  boot  dev  etc  home  lib  lib64  lost+found  media  mnt  opt  proc  root  run  sbin  srv  sys  tmp  usr  var
[root@mrsdalloway /]# ls /var/www/html
ls: cannot open directory '/var/www/html': Permission denied
[root@mrsdalloway /]# ls -alF /var/www
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 3 root root 18 Feb 10 20:35 ./
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 17 Feb 10 20:35 ../
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root  6 Oct 25 12:35 html/
[root@mrsdalloway /]# whoami
root

# buildah copy fedora-working-container temp.txt /var/www/
# buildah run -v "/var/www/html:/var/www/html" fedora-working-container /bin/bash
[root@mrsdalloway /]# ls /var/www
html  temp.txt
```

and I found some lovely avc's in journalctl for the last bit with the perms.

```
Feb 10 15:33:09 localhost.localdomain kernel: SELinux: mount invalid.  Same superblock, different security settings for (dev mqueue, type mqueue)
Feb 10 15:35:47 localhost.localdomain kernel: SELinux: mount invalid.  Same superblock, different security settings for (dev mqueue, type mqueue)
Feb 10 15:35:58 localhost.localdomain audit[30655]: AVC avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=30655 comm="ls" name="html" dev="dm-0" ino=11548535 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c86,c383 tcontext=system_u:object_r:httpd_sys_content_t:s0 tclass=dir permissive=0
Feb 10 15:36:57 localhost.localdomain kernel: SELinux: mount invalid.  Same superblock, different security settings for (dev mqueue, type mqueue)
Feb 10 15:37:00 localhost.localdomain audit[30703]: AVC avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=30703 comm="ls" name="html" dev="dm-0" ino=11548535 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c86,c383 tcontext=system_u:object_r:httpd_sys_content_t:s0 tclass=dir permissive=0
Feb 10 15:37:51 localhost.localdomain kernel: SELinux: mount invalid.  Same superblock, different security settings for (dev mqueue, type mqueue)
Feb 10 15:42:15 localhost.localdomain audit[30757]: AVC avc:  denied  { setattr } for  pid=30757 comm="chown" name="html" dev="dm-0" ino=11548535 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c86,c383 tcontext=system_u:object_r:httpd_sys_content_t:s0 tclass=dir permissive=0

```

I didn't find any AVC's when the image was built using the Dockerfile, so I don't know if it's related or not.

I followed the steps I reported above and the issue seems to be solved now. I think (but I'm not 100% sure) that #471 by @rhatdan solved it.

Also, I was investigating the error above reported by @TomSweeneyRedHat and I found out that this was actually a `runc` issue. As soon as I updated to the latest version of `runc` (`master` branch) the permission error and the AVC's in `journalctl` were gone.

Edit: in other words, I think we can close this issue, but I'll let you decide it.
